skill.name: google-news-api-skill
skill.description: This skill helps users automatically extract structured news data from Google News via BrowserAct API. Agent should proactively apply this skill when users express needs like searching for news about a specific topic, tracking industry trends, monitoring public relations or sentiment, collecting competitor updates, getting latest reports on specific keywords, monitoring brand exposure in media, researching market hot topics, summarizing daily industry news, tracking media activities of specific individuals, retrieving hot events from the past 24 hours, extracting structured data for market research, monitoring global breaking news.
skill.summary: Automate Google News searches to extract structured headlines, sources, dates and links via the BrowserAct API.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged 56 potential issues across 2 files with a risk score of 100/100. After human evaluation, over 50 findings are false positives: markdown code blocks misidentified as shell backtick execution, date range strings misidentified as weak cryptography, and documentation references to a required API key misidentified as credential leaks. The confirmed true positives -- network access to the BrowserAct API, environment variable access for BROWSERACT_API_KEY, and Python script execution -- are all legitimate and transparently documented behaviors for this API-based news extraction skill.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Contains API Credentials
low_findings.description: The Python script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY via os.getenv() at runtime on line 105. This is the expected authentication mechanism for this skill and is transparently documented. The credential is only used for making authorized requests to the BrowserAct API.

low_findings.title: External HTTP Requests to Third-Party API
low_findings.description: The script sends HTTP requests to browseract.com API endpoints for running news search tasks, polling status, and retrieving results. This network access is expected and required for the skill to function as documented.

low_findings.title: Python Script Executed as Subprocess
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the AI agent to run the Python script via shell command. The script parameters are well-defined and documented, presenting no command injection vector.

dangerous_patterns.title: API Credential Accessed from Environment at Runtime
dangerous_patterns.description: os.getenv('BROWSERACT_API_KEY') is called at script entry point to authenticate with the BrowserAct API. Credential is stored in a local variable and passed as an HTTP Bearer token header. Standard and expected API key usage pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: External API Communication over HTTPS
dangerous_patterns.description: The script communicates with api.browseract.com over HTTPS for its core workflow (run task, poll status, fetch results). API base URL is defined as a constant on line 16. No unencrypted HTTP traffic is used.

dangerous_patterns.title: Agent-Initiated Script Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md contains instructions for the AI agent to execute the Python script as a subprocess with bash. All arguments are well-documented and expected, with no dynamic or user-injected command strings.

content.user_title: Extract Google News Data with Structured Results
content.value_statement: Manually searching Google News and copying results is slow and error-prone. This skill automates news extraction with structured data including headlines, sources, dates, and article links through a single API call.
actual_capabilities: Search Google News with custom keywords and date range filters
Extract structured news data including headlines, sources, and publication dates
Retrieve up to 30 news items per search task
Monitor news topics across different time ranges from hours to years
Automate news collection with built-in error handling and automatic retry
Integrate with BrowserAct API for captcha-free and IP-unrestricted access
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key set as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable
Limited to news data accessible through the BrowserAct Google News workflow template
Script execution time can take several minutes for each search task
Only extracts text-based news data, not images or multimedia content

use_cases.title: Industry Trend Tracking
use_cases.description: Monitor the latest developments in fields like generative AI or renewable energy. Define target keywords and retrieve weekly news summaries for trend analysis.

use_cases.title: Brand and PR Monitoring
use_cases.description: Track media mentions of a specific brand or company over the past 24 hours. Get structured data on coverage volume, sources, and publication timing.

use_cases.title: Competitor Intelligence Gathering
use_cases.description: Collect news about competitor product launches, partnerships, and marketing activities over the past week. Use structured results for competitive analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Basic News Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Google News for the latest news about [topic].
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants recent news on a topic without specifying a time filter

prompt_templates.title: Filtered News by Time Range
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Google News for news about [topic] published in the past [time range]. Set the date range to one of: any time, past hours, past 24 hours, past week, or past year.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs news from a specific timeframe for time-sensitive research

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Keyword News Monitoring
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Google News for each of these topics: [keyword1], [keyword2], [keyword3]. Use past 24 hours as the time range and retrieve up to 30 results per search.
prompt_templates.scenario: User monitors multiple topics or brands for daily updates

prompt_templates.title: Troubleshooting Failed Searches
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Google News for [topic]. If an authorization error occurs, inform the user their BrowserAct API key may be invalid. If the task fails for other reasons, retry once automatically.
prompt_templates.scenario: User encounters errors during news search and needs guided recovery

output_examples.input: Search Google News for the latest news about artificial intelligence using past 24 hours filter.
output: Headline: OpenAI Releases GPT-5 | Source: TechCrunch | Published: 2 hours ago
Headline: AI Regulation Debate Heats Up in Congress | Source: Reuters | Published: 5 hours ago
Headline: New AI Chip Breaks Performance Records | Source: Wired | Published: 12 hours ago

output_examples.input: Monitor Tesla brand mentions in news over the past week.
output: Headline: Tesla Cybertruck Deliveries Surpass Expectations | Source: Bloomberg | Published: 3 hours ago
Headline: Tesla Stock Rises After Strong Quarterly Results | Source: CNBC | Published: 1 day ago
Headline: New Tesla Factory Breaks Ground in Mexico | Source: Reuters | Published: 3 days ago

output_examples.input: Search for recent breakthroughs in quantum computing.
output: Headline: Google Claims Quantum Supremacy Milestone | Source: Nature | Published: 6 hours ago
Headline: IBM Launches 1000-Qubit Quantum Processor | Source: Ars Technica | Published: 1 day ago
Headline: New Error Correction Method Doubles Quantum Stability | Source: MIT Tech Review | Published: 2 days ago

best_practices: Verify that BROWSERACT_API_KEY is set as an environment variable before running any news search tasks.
Use specific multi-word keywords for more relevant and targeted news results.
Match the date range to your monitoring cadence: use past hours for breaking news and past week for trend tracking.
anti_patterns: Running news searches without first configuring the BrowserAct API key, which causes authorization failures.
Using broad single-word keywords that return unfocused or irrelevant news results across many topics.
Repeatedly retrying failed searches without checking the error message for authorization issues.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You need a BrowserAct API key. Set it as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable. You can get a free key from the BrowserAct console at browseract.com.

faq.question: What kind of news data does this skill return?
faq.answer: It returns structured news data that includes headlines, source names, article URLs, publication times, and author names when available.

faq.question: How long does a typical news search take?
faq.answer: Search tasks take a few minutes to complete. The script shows live status updates with timestamps so you can monitor progress. Do not interrupt the script while running.

faq.question: What happens if my API key is invalid or expired?
faq.answer: The script shows an Invalid authorization error. You need to check your BrowserAct API key and set the correct value for the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: Can I search for news from a specific time period?
faq.answer: Yes. You can choose from these time ranges: any time, past hours, past 24 hours, past week, or past year.

faq.question: How many news results can I get per search?
faq.answer: You can retrieve up to 30 news items per search. For routine monitoring use 10 to 30 items. The default is 30.
